extraction.one_line_summary: VAN DER POEL Mathieu won the uphill finish in Visegrád after EWAN Caleb crashed touching GIRMAY Biniam's wheel in the final sprint.
tactical_patterns: KÄMNA Lennard attacked solo inside 3km on uphill finish, revealing Bora's willingness to gamble early GC bonus seconds.
NASSEN Lawrence attacked for AG2R before Kämna, showing teams testing whether uphill gradient could create separation before sprint.
ALPECIN controlled final kilometers after EF and Groupama helped chase, indicating sprint teams willing to share pace on hybrid finishes.
EWAN Caleb survived gradient as only pure sprinter, but positioning third wheel proved fatal on steep uphill sprint.

rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: won uphill sprint convincingly as pre-stage favorite, validating strength on 4% gradients at race start
rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: positive: finished second in uphill sprint and took white jersey, immediately competitive against puncheurs at Grand Tour debut
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: positive: rolled third for 4 bonus seconds after crash, opportunistically gaining time despite uphill sprint not suiting pure climbers
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: only pure sprinter to survive gradient but crashed touching wheel in top-3 position, revealing poor bike handling under fatigue
rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: neutral: solo attack gained 10 seconds but caught inside final km, showing form but insufficient gradient for true separation
rider_signals.NASSEN Lawrence: neutral: first to attack for AG2R despite not being original roster selection, suggesting team tactical flexibility
rider_signals.ULISSI Diego: neutral: FORMOLO and VILLELLA led him out but finish position unclear, UAE train intact but execution questionable
rider_signals.CORT Magnus: neutral: EWAN positioned third on his wheel before crash, suggesting EF sacrificed own sprint for leadout work
